symptom: a wisdom tooth that never came through and a jaw that felt oddly full on one side
hook: an unerupted tooth was sealed inside a smooth round bubble deep in the jaw
whatYouSee: A smooth round cyst sits in the jaw wrapped around the crown of a tooth that never erupted.
whyItMatters: The fluid sac keeps the tooth trapped and slowly expands the bone and left alone it can rarely turn into something worse.
treatment: The cyst and the trapped tooth are removed together and the bone heals back in.
takeaway: Sometimes a tooth just gets locked away and forgotten.
igOptions: A. A dentigerous cyst around a buried tooth

condition.keyFindings: a well defined round radiolucent cyst in the jaw wrapped around the crown of an unerupted impacted tooth, over a single clean continuous dental arch

laypersonQuestion: Why would a tooth get trapped in a bubble?
seedHint: Look at what is wrapped around the base of that tooth and ask yourself if bone should look like that.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a wisdom tooth that never came through and a jaw that felt oddly full on one side.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And an unerupted tooth was sealed inside a smooth round bubble deep in the jaw.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Dentigerous cyst

👀 What you see:
A smooth round cyst sits in the jaw wrapped around the crown of a tooth that never erupted.

🦴 Why it matters:
The fluid sac keeps the tooth trapped and slowly expands the bone and left alone it can rarely turn into something worse.

💊 Treatment:

The cyst and the trapped tooth are removed together and the bone heals back in.
